name: Jeffrey Epstein
did: Federal conviction: trafficking of minors for sexual abuse.
Operated abuse network across multiple states and countries.
Used private island (Little St. James), Manhattan mansion, New Mexico ranch.
Victims: dozens of underage girls — some as young as 14.
First plea deal (2008): non-prosecution agreement — widely condemned as corrupt.
Died in federal custody Aug 2019 — ruled suicide.
facts: 2008 Florida conviction: solicitation of prostitution with a minor. Served 13 months.
2008 NPA (non-prosecution agreement): protected co-conspirators — signed by Alex Acosta (later Trump Labor Secretary).
2019 SDNY indictment: sex trafficking of minors.
Flight logs: hundreds of flights on private jets with prominent passengers.
Victim testimonies: 36+ women provided statements to federal investigators.
Properties: Manhattan townhouse, Palm Beach mansion, Little St. James island, Zorro Ranch NM, Paris apartment.
Financial network: unclear source of wealth — possible intelligence connections investigated.
Died in Metropolitan Correctional Center — guards failed to check on him, cameras malfunctioned.
sources: NPA agreement (signed by AUSA, approved by Alex Acosta)

name: Ghislaine Maxwell
said: I never saw any inappropriate behavior.
did: Convicted on 5 of 6 federal counts including sex trafficking of a minor (Dec 2021).
Sentenced to 20 years in federal prison.
Recruited and groomed underage girls for Epstein.
Participated directly in sexual abuse of minors — victim testimony.
facts: Federal conviction: sex trafficking of minor, transporting minor to engage in criminal sexual activity, conspiracy.
Sentenced to 20 years (June 2022).
Four victims testified at trial about recruitment and grooming.
Virginia Giuffre: recruited at age 16 from Mar-a-Lago.
Fled to New Hampshire property, arrested July 2020 by FBI.
Father: Robert Maxwell — media mogul, died under suspicious circumstances 1991.
sources: US v. Maxwell — SDNY trial record and verdict (Dec 2021)
Virginia Giuffre civil complaint and testimony

name: Prince Andrew (Duke of York)
said: I have no recollection of meeting this woman.
did: Settled Virginia Giuffre sexual abuse lawsuit for reported $12M+ (Feb 2022).
Photographed with arm around 17-year-old Giuffre at Maxwell's London home.
Visited Epstein after 2008 conviction — photographed together in Central Park 2010.
Stripped of military titles and royal patronages by Queen Elizabeth (Jan 2022).
facts: Civil settlement with Virginia Giuffre — undisclosed amount, reported $12M+ (Feb 2022).
Photo evidence: Andrew with arm around Giuffre (age 17) at Maxwell's London residence.
Giuffre testimony: trafficked to Andrew by Epstein/Maxwell on multiple occasions.
Andrew visited Epstein in New York AFTER 2008 conviction — stayed at mansion.
BBC Newsnight interview (2019): widely seen as disastrous — claimed he 'didn't sweat.'
Stripped of all military affiliations, royal patronages, use of HRH by the Queen.
sources: Giuffre v. Andrew complaint and testimony

name: Jean-Luc Brunel
said: I am innocent of all charges.
did: Charged with rape of minors and sexual harassment in France.
Supplied young models to Epstein — victim testimonies.
MC2 modeling agency: used as pipeline for young girls.
Found dead in prison Feb 2022 — ruled suicide, before trial.
facts: French prosecutors charged Brunel with rape of minors, sexual harassment (2020).
Multiple victims testified he supplied girls as young as 15 to Epstein.
MC2 Model Management: co-founded with Epstein funding — used as recruitment vehicle.
Arrested at Charles de Gaulle airport Dec 2020.
Found hanged in Paris prison Feb 2022 — before trial could proceed.
Flight logs: frequent passenger on Epstein's planes.

name: Alex Acosta
said: The plea deal was appropriate given the evidence at the time.
did: As US Attorney, approved sweetheart plea deal for Epstein (2008 NPA).
Deal gave Epstein 13 months in private wing of county jail with work release.
Protected unnamed co-conspirators from prosecution.
Failed to notify victims as required by law — federal judge ruled NPA violated Crime Victims' Rights Act.
Later appointed Trump's Secretary of Labor — resigned when NPA became public scandal (2019).
facts: 2008 NPA: Epstein avoided federal charges, served 13 months county jail, 6 days/week work release.
Federal judge (Kenneth Marra, 2019): NPA violated Crime Victims' Rights Act — victims not notified.
Deal protected 'any potential co-conspirators' from federal prosecution.
OPR (Office of Professional Responsibility): found Acosta used 'poor judgment' but not professional misconduct.
Resigned as Labor Secretary July 2019 after NPA details became national news.
sources: Judge Marra ruling — Crime Victims' Rights Act violation

name: Les Wexner
role: CEO, L Brands (Victoria's Secret) / Epstein Associate
said: I severed ties when I learned of his activities.
did: Gave Epstein sweeping power of attorney over his finances.
Gifted Epstein the Manhattan mansion (worth $77M).
Epstein managed Wexner's money for over a decade.
Relationship continued years after Epstein's 2008 conviction.
facts: Power of attorney: Epstein had authority over all of Wexner's financial affairs.
Manhattan townhouse: transferred from Wexner to Epstein for $0 (property records).
Epstein claimed Wexner was his only known billionaire client.
Wexner's spokesperson: Wexner severed ties in 2007 — but property transfer and financial records suggest continued relationship.
No criminal charges filed against Wexner.
sources: Power of attorney documents (referenced in NYT investigation)
